completed_tasks.name: Builder types + Zustand stores + route stubs
Save As + unsaved guard + config serialization + checkpoint
BuilderChartContent uses SQL execute via managed chart/dataset
Builder blueprint aesthetic (toolbar, canvas, empty state, panel, title)
Elegant signature art — flowing bezier curves per dashboard

human_actions_pending.action: Verify phase 08 human UAT items from 08-VERIFICATION.md
human_actions_pending.context: Verifier marked phase complete with status: human_needed. 5 items need manual testing: end-to-end create/save flow, WYSIWYG live panel rendering, config round-trip in edit mode, filter config dialog, unsaved changes guard.

decisions.decision: Dashboard view/edit routes use useDashboardConfig (flat endpoint) not useManagedDashboard
decisions.rationale: The managed endpoint wraps config in a field that's in DATA_KEYS, so snake_case inside stays un-transformed by api-client. Both endpoints read the same recviz_dashboards table, so user-created dashboards still work.

decisions.decision: BuilderChartContent fetches managed chart + dataset and executes SQL via /api/sql/execute
decisions.rationale: The old path used useDataSourceQuery which calls /api/data-sources/{id}/query — that expects legacy config data source IDs, not managed dataset UUIDs. Same pattern as chart-library-card and chart-detail-panel.

decisions.decision: Dashboard library cards use elegant signature art (flowing bezier curves) not blueprint mini-maps
decisions.rationale: User feedback: blueprint mini-maps were busy and technical. Refined minimalist direction inspired by Linear/album art. Each card gets a unique bezier curve via FNV-1a ID hash — same aesthetic, unique fingerprint per card. dashboard-mini-map.tsx is still in codebase but unused.

decisions.decision: Builder uses architectural-blueprint aesthetic (dot grid, corner ticks, DSH/{id} mono label, SVG blueprint illustration for empty state)
decisions.rationale: The builder is a creative workspace — the blueprint theme signals construction mode and differentiates it from the refined minimalist library view. Two modes, two moods.

next_action: Either: (a) run /gsd-verify-work 8 to walk through the 5 human UAT items via Playwright, (b) run /gsd-discuss-phase 9 to start Phase 9 (Sharing and Views), or (c) wait for further user feedback on the polished builder/library.
context_notes: Phase 08 Dashboard Builder is fully built and verified. All 10 plans completed, 3 post-verification bug fixes applied, and 2 rounds of design polish completed. Library cards now use elegant signature art (flowing bezier curves unique per dashboard). Builder uses blueprint aesthetic (dot grid canvas, architectural corner ticks, DSH/id monospace label, distinctive empty state illustration). Both TLM Stats and Chart Showcase dashboards verified working in view mode. Chart add in builder verified working via Playwright. The unused dashboard-mini-map.tsx component remains in the codebase — can be repurposed for a detail view later or deleted if unneeded.
